audio_script:
Mark: Hi Lisa! I haven't seen you for a long time. Where were you? 
Lisa: Oh, hi Mark! Last month, I went to Paris for a short holiday. 
Mark: Really? That's amazing! Did you see the Eiffel Tower? 
Lisa: Yes, I did! I climbed to the second floor. It was very crowded but the view was beautiful. 
Mark: What was your favorite part of the trip? 
Lisa: The food! I ate a lot of delicious croissants and cheese. It was an unforgettable memory.

questions.question: Where did Lisa go for her holiday?
What was Lisa's favorite part of the trip?

audio_script:
Tom: Hey Jenny! Have you finished the project yet? 
Jenny: Not yet, Tom. I have been very busy lately. 
Tom: What did you do last week? 
Jenny: Well, on Monday, I traveled to a conference in Berlin. I met some interesting people there. Then, on Wednesday, I came back and started writing my report. 
Tom: Have you written everything? 
Jenny: I have written half of it. I will finish it tomorrow.

questions.question: Why hasn't Jenny finished the project yet?
What did Jenny do on Monday?

audio_script:
Agent: Good morning! How can I help you? 
Customer: Good morning. I am going to plan a holiday for my family next summer. 
Agent: Great! Where are you going to go? 
Customer: We haven't decided yet. Maybe we will go to the mountains. 
Agent: If you like nature, the Alps will be a perfect choice. 
Customer: That sounds wonderful. We are going to need a big cabin because there are five of us.

Mark: Hey Sarah! Where are you going for your holiday? 
Sarah: I'm not sure. I'm thinking about London or Rome. 
Mark: Well, London is much bigger than Rome, but Rome is sunnier. 
Sarah: True. London is more expensive, but it has the most amazing museums. 
Mark: That's right. However, the food in Rome is better than the food in London. 
Sarah: You're right. Rome is also more relaxing. I think I will choose Rome this year!

questions.question: Which city does Mark think is sunnier?

audio_script:
Doctor: Hello, Mr. Smith. How are you feeling today? 
Mr. Smith: Not very well, doctor. I have a terrible headache and my throat hurts. 
Doctor: I see. You should drink plenty of water and get some rest. 
Mr. Smith: Should I take any medicine? 
Doctor: Yes, I will give you a prescription. But you shouldn't go to work for two days. You need to stay at home and sleep.

questions.question: What is Mr. Smith's problem?
options: A headache and a sore throat
questions.correct_answer: A headache and a sore throat

audio_script:
Host: Welcome to 'Quick Reviews'. Today, I'm talking about the new sci-fi movie, 'Star Journey'. 
Host: The story is simply incredible. It's much more exciting than the previous movie. The actors perform beautifully, especially the main character. 
Host: However, the movie is extremely long. It's almost three hours! Some parts move too slowly. 
Host: Overall, it is a fantastic visual experience. If you like space adventures, you should definitely watch it.

questions.question: What kind of movie is 'Star Journey'?
What is a negative point about the movie according to the host?

audio_script:
Interviewer: Good morning. Please, take a seat. 
Applicant: Thank you. 
Interviewer: So, I have read your CV. Can you tell me why you want to work for our company? 
Applicant: Well, your company is very innovative. I have always wanted to work in a dynamic environment like this. 
Interviewer: I see. What are your main strengths? 
Applicant: I am very organized and I work well in a team. I also have good communication skills.

questions.question: Why does the applicant want to work for the company?
options: Because it is close to home.

questions.question: What is ONE of the applicant's strengths?
